items.title: Where Are The Aircraft Carriers: July 20, 2026
items.summary: The U.S. conducted a weekend wave of strikes against Iran and announced a new round today, while the reinstated blockade of Iranian ports approaches the one-week mark. The post Where Are The Aircraft Carriers: July 20, 2026 appeared first on TWZ .

items.title: Rebuilding America’s Critical Minerals Base

items.summary: Cultural decolonization advances alongside unprecedented Western partnerships.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.summary: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.summary: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: Ukraine shut a quarter of Russia’s grain exports—the world’s wheat money now flows to Russia’s competitors
items.summary: Ukraine’s Azov campaign built a paradox: the world pays more for wheat because Russia cannot ship it—and the windfall flows to Russia’s competitors.
items.details: Ukraine’s Azov campaign built a paradox: the world pays more for wheat because Russia cannot ship it—and the windfall flows to Russia’s competitors.

items.title: The US military campaign to stop drug trafficking has failed. It’s time to move on.
items.summary: The mission has not succeeded in its core goals, and it’s using up military manpower and munitions at a time when the US is stretched on both, writes Daniel R. DePetris.
items.details: The mission has not succeeded in its core goals, and it’s using up military manpower and munitions at a time when the US is stretched on both, writes Daniel R. DePetris.

items.title: US eyes new tariffs as existing trade duties near expiration
items.summary: US Trade Rep Jamieson Greer hints at new tariffs on 60 trading partners as Trump's temporary tariffs expire on Friday.
items.details: US Trade Rep Jamieson Greer hints at new tariffs on 60 trading partners as Trump's temporary tariffs expire on Friday.

items.title: EUKOR Completes First Green Methanol Bunkering for New Shaper-Class Car Carrier in Shanghai
items.summary: EUKOR Car Carriers has completed its first green methanol bunkering operation, marking another milestone in the commercial rollout of methanol-powered vehicle carriers as the industry works to expand the availability...

items.title: India Summons Russian Envoy After Four Sailors Killed in Ukraine
items.summary: India called in Russia’s top diplomat on Tuesday to convey New Delhi’s “unequivocal condemnation” of a Russian attack on a vessel departing the Ukrainian port of Odesa that killed four Indian sailors and left another critically injured in the hospital.
items.details: India called in Russia’s top diplomat on Tuesday to convey New Delhi’s “unequivocal condemnation” of a Russian attack on a vessel departing the Ukrainian port of Odesa that killed four Indian sailors and left another critically injured in the hospital.

items.title: France Is Poised to Restrict Social Media for Children
items.summary: Its new law, which would be the first of its kind enacted by a European country, would ban access to some accounts for anyone under 15.
items.details: Its new law, which would be the first of its kind enacted by a European country, would ban access to some accounts for anyone under 15.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.summary: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.
items.details: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.title: Boeing on track for 2028 Air Force One delivery, costs rise
items.summary: Boeing remains on track to deliver two new Air Force One jets in 2028, but meeting that target will require additional spending.
items.details: Boeing remains on track to deliver two new Air Force One jets in 2028, but meeting that target will require additional spending.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.
items.details: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

items.title: Zelensky's office welcomes pro-Trump influencer Laura Loomer's visit to Ukraine
items.summary: "It is really important that Laura Loomer is in Ukraine and sees things with her own eyes as they are," Zelensky's office said.
items.details: "It is really important that Laura Loomer is in Ukraine and sees things with her own eyes as they are," Zelensky's office said.

items.title: Trump Escalates Canada Tariffs as Mark Carney Holds Firm
items.summary: The president’s new tariffs on a top U.S. trading partner aim at gaining concessions, but the Canadian government, and the public, have been bracing for the escalation.
items.details: The president’s new tariffs on a top U.S. trading partner aim at gaining concessions, but the Canadian government, and the public, have been bracing for the escalation.

items.title: US-Iran tensions escalate in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Recent military exchanges have increased tensions in the critical shipping lane
items.details: The Strait of Hormuz has seen increased military activity as US and Iranian forces exchange strikes. Tanker traffic has slowed to two-month lows amid safety concerns. Both nations claim defensive actions while accusing the other of escalation.

items.title: Russia launches major missile strike on Kyiv
items.summary: One of the largest ballistic missile attacks since the war began
items.details: Russian forces launched a significant ballistic missile barrage targeting Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted many missiles, but several hit residential areas causing casualties and infrastructure damage.

items.title: Ukraine reports success against Russian Black Sea fleet
items.summary: Ukrainian drones strike 12 Russian vessels in Black Sea operations
items.details: Ukraine's military announced successful drone strikes against 12 Russian vessels including cargo ships, tankers, and military support ships in the Black Sea. The operation demonstrates Ukraine's growing asymmetric naval capabilities.

items.title: China increases military activity near Taiwan
items.summary: People's Liberation Army conducts drills near Taiwan Strait
items.details: Chinese military aircraft and vessels have increased operations near Taiwan, conducting what Beijing calls 'routine training' but Taipei views as intimidation. Taiwan has responded with increased readiness and international appeals for support.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic at two-month low
items.summary: Shipping data shows reduced tanker transits amid US-Iran tensions
items.details: The number of tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz fell to the lowest level in two months as renewed US and Iranian strikes raise safety concerns. Shipping companies report increased insurance costs and rerouting around the Arabian Peninsula.
